lines.text: BROOKLYN 1. N. Y.
Latest reports listed
analyzed in Flying Saucer
Review, printed Space Age
April 6 1954
Federal Bureau of Investigation
Pennsylvania and 9th
Washington DC
Gentlemen:
Further to my registered letter 33520 of March 1, listing persons we
planned contacting for the new CIVILIAN SAUCER INTELLIGENCE, to discuss
Saucers, I am sending a second report on membership. I have reached much
definite impressions since then, on some members, and enclose these for what
they are worth.
From her actions, general attitudes, and methods, I am almost positive
[REDACTED] is part of some other group, possibly subversive, investigating
the field for possible civilian information on the subject.
From her attitude, toward [REDACTED] and [REDACTED] her contacting me twice
after I had written [REDACTED] twice after long activity, his unusual willingness
to cooperate with our group, different from his previous closemouthed attitude,
I am almost positive he is part of the same group, and either directs her or
the entire group in New York City. He does a great deal of travelling from
New York to Washington and Baltimore, and may be some sort of courier.
A clipping has come in that [REDACTED] also previously listed,
states he is a research 'Assistante Professor' to PROFESSOR [REDACTED]
in Albuquerque, now studying 'Meteoritics'. I have nothing as to Professor
[REDACTED] background other than he has given out some peculiarly mis-informed
ideas on the subject in recent years.
These include: a widely circulated release that the Green fireballs were
not 'natural' phenomena, and probably were American secret weapons; an article
in People Today that saucers and fireballs were probably coming from northern
Russian bases; a news release that flying saucers over Northern Japan in
Jan. 1953, were probably Russian guided missiles, contrary to all known
performances of missiles.
After sending [REDACTED] some special information on saucer reports,
he mentioned after long delay that he had been in Cambridge, Mass. Whether
or not there is a connection, PROFESSOR [REDACTED] who wrote a deliberately
misinformative and debunking book on flying saucers, lives near Cambridge
while working at Harvard. According to some persons who have supposedly met
his daughter, they are unquestionably communists or fellow travellers. However,
I am not sure of the character of the persons who [unclear] must state
is only hearsay evidence.
